text:
There once was a town made entirely of dough. The people rolled around, they baked into shapes, and they even had a bakery in the middle. Every day was the same: roll out of bed, grab some honeyglaze, and head to work sculpting desks or houses. No one knew what they'd really look like; they existed purely as delicious treats.

One unusual thing happened one morning. A giant rat started roaming around town. It wasn't just big—it had the fear-inspiring glee of a baker with a giant flour bag. It liked to chew on dough, though it clearly wasn't human.

People were scared. The rat skipped right past them in the dough ball form it preferred. What could they do? Everyone decided on a daring plan. They'd each give it a tiny piece of their breakfast. Sure enough, that giant rat was irresistibly delicious.

So, every morning, they'd sneak crumbs out their porches before going to work. The rat grew fond
